questions.q: In which Indian film industry is the director Adoor Gopalakrishnan a leading figure?
questions.explanation: Adoor Gopalakrishnan is a celebrated Malayalam filmmaker, often called the father of the Malayalam new wave.

questions.q: Which 1972 film marked Adoor Gopalakrishnan's feature directorial debut?
questions.explanation: Swayamvaram (1972) was Adoor Gopalakrishnan's debut feature and is regarded as a founding work of the Malayalam new wave.

questions.q: Adoor Gopalakrishnan is widely described as the father of which cinematic movement?
questions.explanation: Adoor Gopalakrishnan is regarded as the father of the Malayalam new wave for pioneering a serious, art-cinema tradition in Kerala.

questions.q: In which year was the filmmaker Adoor Gopalakrishnan born?
questions.explanation: Adoor Gopalakrishnan was born in 1941 in present-day Kerala.

questions.q: Adoor Gopalakrishnan was a co-founder of which pioneering film society and cooperative in Kerala?
questions.explanation: Adoor Gopalakrishnan co-founded the Chitralekha Film Cooperative, a key institution for serious filmmaking in Kerala.

questions.q: Which of India's highest cinema honours did Adoor Gopalakrishnan receive in 2004?
questions.explanation: Adoor Gopalakrishnan was awarded the Dadasaheb Phalke Award, India's highest film honour, in 2004.

questions.q: Adoor Gopalakrishnan is best known for working primarily in which language?
questions.explanation: Adoor Gopalakrishnan made nearly all of his films in Malayalam, the language of Kerala.

questions.q: Which prestigious national award has Adoor Gopalakrishnan won multiple times for direction?
options: National Film Award for Best Director
questions.explanation: Adoor Gopalakrishnan has won the National Film Award for Best Director several times over his career.

questions.q: Adoor Gopalakrishnan's films are most associated with which kind of cinema?
questions.explanation: Adoor Gopalakrishnan is a leading figure of Indian art and parallel cinema, known for realistic, contemplative storytelling.

questions.q: In which Indian state was Adoor Gopalakrishnan born and where he set most of his films?
questions.explanation: Adoor Gopalakrishnan was born in Kerala and rooted nearly all of his films in Kerala's society and landscape.

questions.q: Which 1981 Adoor Gopalakrishnan film, whose title means 'The Rat-Trap', portrays a feudal landlord clinging to a dying way of life?
questions.explanation: Elippathayam (1981), meaning 'The Rat-Trap', is one of Adoor Gopalakrishnan's most acclaimed films.

questions.q: Which premier Indian film institute did Adoor Gopalakrishnan attend before becoming a director?
options: Film and Television Institute of India, Pune
L. V. Prasad Film Academy, Chennai
questions.explanation: Adoor Gopalakrishnan studied at the Film and Television Institute of India (FTII) in Pune, graduating in scriptwriting and direction.

questions.q: Adoor Gopalakrishnan's 1990 film Mathilukal ('Walls') is based on the writings of which Malayalam author?
questions.explanation: Mathilukal (1990) is adapted from the prison-set autobiographical writings of Vaikom Muhammad Basheer.

questions.q: Which celebrated Malayalam actor played the lonely prisoner in Adoor Gopalakrishnan's Mathilukal (1990)?
questions.explanation: Mammootty starred as the imprisoned writer in Adoor Gopalakrishnan's Mathilukal (1990), a role that won him a National Film Award.

questions.q: Which 1977 Adoor Gopalakrishnan film follows the journey of a passive, drifting man named Sankarankutty?
questions.explanation: Kodiyettam (1977) traces the maturing of its aimless protagonist Sankarankutty, played by Bharath Gopi.

questions.q: Which Malayalam actor won wide acclaim for the lead role in Adoor Gopalakrishnan's Kodiyettam (1977)?
questions.explanation: Bharath Gopi gave a landmark performance as Sankarankutty in Adoor Gopalakrishnan's Kodiyettam (1977).

questions.q: Adoor Gopalakrishnan's 1993 film Vidheyan ('The Servile'), about a tyrannical master and his servant, starred which actor as the cruel landlord?
questions.explanation: Mammootty played the brutal landlord Bhaskara Patelar in Adoor Gopalakrishnan's Vidheyan (1993).

questions.q: Which 2002 Adoor Gopalakrishnan film, whose title means 'Shadow Kill', centres on a hangman troubled by guilt?
questions.explanation: Nizhalkkuthu (2002), meaning 'Shadow Kill', follows a state executioner haunted by his role.

questions.q: Apart from making films, Adoor Gopalakrishnan is also widely known as a maker of what kind of non-fiction works?
questions.explanation: Adoor Gopalakrishnan has directed several documentaries, including works on Kerala's traditional performing arts.

questions.q: Adoor Gopalakrishnan came from a family with strong ties to which traditional Kerala performing art?
questions.explanation: Adoor Gopalakrishnan grew up in a family closely linked to Kathakali, Kerala's highly stylised dance-drama tradition.

questions.q: Adoor Gopalakrishnan's debut feature Swayamvaram (1972) won the National Film Award for Best Feature Film in which language category that year?
questions.explanation: Swayamvaram (1972) won the National Film Award for Best Feature Film, a major recognition for Malayalam cinema.

questions.q: Which civilian honour from the Government of India was conferred on Adoor Gopalakrishnan, recognising his contribution to cinema?
questions.explanation: Adoor Gopalakrishnan was awarded the Padma Vibhushan, one of India's highest civilian honours.

questions.q: Which 1987 Adoor Gopalakrishnan film, structured around an unreliable narrator, has a title meaning 'Monologue' or 'Thereafter'?
questions.explanation: Anantaram (1987) is noted for its complex, subjective narration told through a troubled young man's recollections.

questions.q: Adoor Gopalakrishnan's 1984 film Mukhamukham ('Face to Face') sparked debate for its critical look at which political movement in Kerala?
questions.explanation: Mukhamukham (1984) drew controversy for its reflective, critical portrayal of the Communist movement in Kerala.

questions.q: Adoor Gopalakrishnan's 1995 film Kathapurushan ('The Man of the Story') traces a protagonist's life against the backdrop of which period of Kerala's history?
questions.explanation: Kathapurushan (1995) follows its hero's growth amid the political and social transformations of post-independence Kerala.

questions.q: Adoor Gopalakrishnan's 2007 film Naalu Pennungal ('Four Women') is based on stories by which Malayalam writer?
questions.explanation: Naalu Pennungal (2007) adapts short stories by Thakazhi Sivasankara Pillai into four episodes about women's lives.

questions.q: Adoor Gopalakrishnan directed comparatively few feature films over his long career, with the total numbering roughly how many?
questions.explanation: Adoor Gopalakrishnan is known for a small but acclaimed body of around a dozen feature films made carefully over decades.

questions.q: At which international film festival did Adoor Gopalakrishnan's Mathilukal win the FIPRESCI (international critics) prize in 1990?
questions.explanation: Mathilukal (1990) won the FIPRESCI critics' prize at the Venice Film Festival, marking Adoor Gopalakrishnan's global recognition.

questions.q: Adoor Gopalakrishnan's later film Oru Pennum Randaanum (2008) is a portmanteau work adapting short stories by which Malayalam author?
questions.explanation: Oru Pennum Randaanum (2008) brings together adaptations of Malayalam short stories by Karoor Neelakanta Pillai.

questions.q: Within Indian parallel cinema, Adoor Gopalakrishnan is often discussed alongside which Bengali master as a fellow auteur of regional art film?
questions.explanation: Adoor Gopalakrishnan is frequently ranked alongside Satyajit Ray as one of India's foremost art-cinema directors.
